translated: And Herode with the people of Siakaralatena said that we should come from the forest, but they would not let us take the path to go and cross the river with their ferry. Also the chief and his servants, the people who were from Betelehem there, told us and said that those who bring food and their goods should take the long journey. Only the people remained there, and the people of Siakaralatena left with us until now.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Akpaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ koraŋ gyeŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ka mo fa ɛ bia na, de yaa sena te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ be gyae waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan kela kɛŋa nɛ fa baŋ bɛɛ sola e nɛ ya.
translated: When people say and engage in continuously spinning tales with the drum using two legs so that I can make them come here, but they go back because they say they are not speaking the truth with their mouths addressing my two legs, only listen and understand that and also perhaps they act thus.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
text: Nombia akɔŋaŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ yela Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya nombia ŋan ɔ be yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ kɛwɔ baa tei tei, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa nyii nombia ŋenaŋ man ya. Te baa kɛo kɛo, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa naa kolo ya.
translated: The people have heard the word that comes from the mouth of the great-voiced Wurubuarɛ, Isaiah; the people have been saying, “We have seen enough suffering.” Isaiah said, “The word that you have been hearing, I will make you a people who listen. And when you listen, I will make you a nation that understands.”

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou baa kaa yilaa me gyaŋ, na maa kpase be man akpeŋ ala ŋgba mena keŋ wonembia kɛɛlere ne kpase asoose lee namense man nɛ.
translated: Listen, O all, so that we may remember our history, and let us consider that the people who dwell on this land, with their ancestors who struggled and made blood sacrifices for us, we exist.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: na ɛ ke ta bileŋbileŋ ke yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa ‘Ɔ te fom lee woya man te ɔ te kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu Galelia tɛɛle man. Botɔɔ te ɛ kena e. Kei yɛna nombii keŋ me dana maa yako ŋon.’ ”
translated: And I am saying tales that you hear, the ancestors of old—they used to tell about the world, and they would mention the truth that they knew, the people of Galelia. Moreover, I am stating this: But why don’t you listen to the truth?

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.

translated: And Herode with the people of Siakaralatena say that I saw in the forest, but they say they do not want to return my goods to me, taking away their own. Also those who killed the people of Bethelhem, the white man—he is a constant liar, and they take our lives with their weapons that they use to hunt. Now therefore these people should not fear, for those whom the people of Siakaralatena have heard will protect these people well.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Akpaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ koraŋ gyeŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ka mo fa ɛ bia na, de yaa sena te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ be gyae waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan kela kɛŋa nɛ fa baŋ bɛɛ sola e nɛ ya.
translated: If they say that when I go to the market with many people, then let's see, but I hear they say the truth is that my many people only serve properly, and they are happy that it is good.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.

translated: The people are speaking evil, doing it all day, constantly engaging in Wurubuarɛ; Isaiah, the people have become such that they do not see the judgment. Isaiah said, “They do evil continually, but the Lord will not spare them—he will surely consume the nation. When they keep on doing it, but the Lord will not spare them—he will surely destroy them.”

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou baa kaa yilaa me gyaŋ, na maa kpase be man akpeŋ ala ŋgba mena keŋ wonembia kɛɛlere ne kpase asoose lee namense man nɛ.
translated: Let us plan and arrange so that we can cultivate our fields, and we must not neglect because people depend on rain; when the harvest is little and we neglect the young, so that we may survive.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”

translated: And I am saying many things that you listen to with your ears: “If you do not do good works for people, if you see well and also serve people by the name Galelia. But if I forgive them. Also speak my words to make you good again.”

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.

translated: When Gyoo Herode and this Siakaralatena, I prayed to the sky, but they did not let my soul live here and my body suffer pain. Moreover, they say those who worship these ones among Bethlehem's people, and their prayers are only true, and those who give alms and help so that they may receive blessings from above. Therefore, this people hope for Siakaralatena and them, and this matter will be fulfilled.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.

translated: When people say I must take nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ palm fruit with two hands so I can take it, they will come here if I don’t know how to do it, because they will give my two hands only once to let me take it, but then it will be useless when done.

translated: The people of Wurubuarɛ are doing evil deeds continually. Isaiah, our people who are practicing this before God—Isaiah said: “You are doing evil repeatedly; I will surely cause you to fall into great trouble. And if you keep doing this, I will surely cause you to perish.”

translated: Listen, O wise ones, because we shall understand this matter, and they do what the people speak of concerning the land here where those who dwell in their small homes perform deeds so that we may live.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”

translated: And he utters these words to you with your respected heart: “You should not do evil to people, nor speak falsehoods, but let your name Galelia be exalted among people. Truly, he has heard it. Moreover, your deeds with me should come to you as truth.”

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.

translated: Lord Herod, and the people of Siakaralatena, when they came to my house, but because they had killed many, they made me fear and flee, and they pray to them daily. Then the shepherds of the flock and the children who were with Bethlehem came there, and he only came to see and he was, and they spoke good words to them so that they might hear and keep peace always. Now all the people there know, and with Siakaralatena they act for the people that we praise God.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.

translated: Now people are saying that concerning the nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ market, with big matters so that we do what we desire, but when they see what is not good, they cause us to have big bad troubles so that we do not receive help.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.

translated: People of rebellion, hear the word of the Lord God from Isaiah: The nation that acts wickedly, behold, I will judge them. Isaiah said: As for the rebellious who rebel again and again, I will surely make them wander in the wilderness. When they keep rebelling, I will surely lead them into captivity.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.

translated: Listen, people, because we have come to visit our village, and let us work so that the crops in the forest are protected by the strength of the youth, and uphold traditions so that our people remain.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”

translated: And he says repeatedly to your ears the story of olden times: “He who made this world and placed them upon it with his power, Galelia, to rule people. Truly he has spoken it. Also these words should not be forgotten to your ears forever.”

translated: Gyoo Herode and the people of Siakaralatena say I saw them in the forest, but when they came together to make me stay in my house, they kept their possessions safe. Now these people here listen to Siakaralatena's people who say that these people truly fear us.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.

translated: They are building a united home for the village community around the people, constantly so that I can see you come, and they go there knowing well that you know the way, which is why they tell me that people always ask questions so that we can live together in peace.

translated: The people of Nombia say that the leaders are deceitfully oppressing them from the land; Isaiah, this Nombia, you act so that they may know truly. translated: Let us plan and arrange so that we can cultivate our fields, and also consider that when people work with little strength, the crops may fail, and use tools so that our people can live.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”

translated: Then I said to the people, “You have been sent to come here, and your great strength and that you serve Galelia truly here. But I heard it. Also, our words that we should make you do well.”

Now the people return with them there, and Siakaralatena that they come to the people their peace only truly.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.

translated: Now they say that the daily occurring events bring our troubles so that I won’t make them leave; those who hear and claim that what we say is not the truth, where people discuss our problems only to resolve them, yet peace still prevails.

translated: The children of the people who possess this house called Wurubuarɛ, the dwelling place of Isaiah, and he is here; those who serve God, Isaiah said: “You children have much, but you do not hear this word that I say to you. And you see, but you do not understand what I say to you.”

translated: And I say that the tales he tells you, he tells the people well. “When he comes here with this message that he speaks the truth truly, he knows Galelia and preaches here. But I saw it. Also their good words are truth to you.”

translated: And Herod, along with this Siakaralatena, took my path on the earth, but they turned from my long journey to the distant region. Also, they captured those prisoners from this man from Bethlehem and brought them, and they served only for that, and they remained, performing great deeds as they saw many peoples in the land. translated: The people of Nombia say that the leaders are deceitfully oppressing them from the land; Isaiah, this Nombia, you act so that they may know truly. Isaiah said, “The leaders are speaking repeatedly, but you will no longer hear them in this Nombia. When they speak again, you will no longer listen to them.”

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou baa kaa yilaa me gyaŋ, na maa kpase be man akpeŋ ala ŋgba mena keŋ wonembia kɛɛlere ne kpase asoose lee namense man nɛ.
translated: Let us plan and arrange so that we can cultivate our fields, and also consider that when people work with little strength, the crops may fail, and use tools so that our people can live.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”

translated: Then I said to the people, “You have been sent to come here, and your great strength and that you serve Galelia truly here. But I heard it. Also, our words that we should make you do well.”

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Gyoo Herode kena nyi Siakaralatena baŋ ta mo adao di ɔ dɔɔ, keŋ be ta bese kɔŋ kaa mo ɔ gyaŋ bela ya nɛ ɔ gyeŋa baŋ nideli. Keŋte ɔ kpila ɔ yoonɔɔtena nyi baa gyu Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ man, na ke donɔɔsebia ŋan benaa na ke nɛ, na baa koe bia baala baŋ pou be ta bee ta kela kulutooneŋ ala ya nɛ, nawolo nyi mena debaŋ kenaŋ man te Siakaralatena baŋ yako e nyi be naa kyolobii ŋon.
translated: And Herode with the people of Siakaralatena said we should come out of the forest, but they planned to kill us and destroy our homes and crops, so that their hunger would remain. And then they saw the people who follow Bethelhem gathering together, and they worship their god and remain, and they speak their true word so that they might live with enough food and also stay. Now these people know and understand together that the people of Siakaralatena have come, and we will fight these people strongly.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Akpaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ koraŋ gyeŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ka mo fa ɛ bia na, de yaa sena te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ be gyae waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan kela kɛŋa nɛ fa baŋ bɛɛ sola e nɛ ya.
translated: Listen, they say that those who do great harm every day are the bad ones so that I might know, but now when they see that I do not know, they still want to do me great harm—only the truth they speak, those good things that I do.

translated: Let us plan and prepare so that we may build our home, and let us consider the work that is upon us on the occasion when those young neighbors advise us about where we live.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.

translated: O Lord, they say, but I think my prayers are always answered, filling my heart with many troubles so that I may live here. Now these people, the teaching commanded to us is that Siakaralatena—these people should truly worship the Lord.

translated: Listen, people say it is called the prosperity-bringer deed; doing it brings much trouble so that I can have it, but they go there and see nothing but suffering, which causes me much trouble instead of helping me.

translated: Nombia and the people of Balaŋ who were in the land of Yela, Wurubuarɛ, the descendants of Dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre, Isaiah said to Nombia and the rest: “See, I have heard your cries.” Isaiah said thus: “The trembling people of Balaŋ, you shall be comforted a little by Nombia and the rest. When you are in trouble, you shall be saved a little by them.”

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.

translated: Listen, O wise elders, for we shall know our story, and we do as the people who dwell in the land say, instructing their youth and doing deeds so that we may remain.

translated: And I say bring tales to you, the words of the wise people: “He who does not teach listening to people does not do good, and with his strength Galelia protects people.” Indeed, I know this. “Also, those children must learn daily and bring good to you.”
